introSection: Big Dipper is a wooden roller coaster located at Camden Park in Huntington, West Virginia, USA. Big Dipper is one of two roller coasters in the park built by the National Amusement Device Company. It is also only one of three surviving roller coasters built by the company. Big Dipper is one of only two coasters to run full-sized NAD Century Flyer trains. The other coaster to run these trains is Thunderbolt at Kennywood in West Mifflin, Pennsylvania, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content: The ride was awarded ACE Roller Coaster Landmark status by the American Coaster Enthusiasts on May 12, 2019 at the 2019 ACE Preservation Conference.

fullText:
Big Dipper is a wooden roller coaster located at Camden Park in Huntington, West Virginia, USA. Big Dipper is one of two roller coasters in the park built by the National Amusement Device Company. It is also only one of three surviving roller coasters built by the company. Big Dipper is one of only two coasters to run full-sized NAD Century Flyer trains. The other coaster to run these trains is Thunderbolt at Kennywood in West Mifflin, Pennsylvania, USA.

The ride was awarded ACE Roller Coaster Landmark status by the American Coaster Enthusiasts on May 12, 2019 at the 2019 ACE Preservation Conference.
